text: Introduction The popularity of research in machine learning (ML) has increased exponentially in the last few decades. However, a large number of researchers without a statistics background are not able to fully understand the methodology of evaluating ML models and their comparison. This survey provides an in-depth review of various evaluation metrics that are used to assess the performance of ML models for several supervised learning tasks such as binary, multi-class and multi-label classification, regression, image segmentation, object detection and information retrieval. It discusses the selection of statistical tests and provides insight into the generation of evaluation metrics for sufficient data to determine the statistical significance. The conducted experiments demonstrate the use cases for evaluating the performance of different types of convolutional neural networks (CNNs) models in assessing lung infections from X-ray images and malignant tumors from positron emission tomography (PET) scans.
